title: Pleopeltis × tricholepis
intro: Pleopeltis × tricholepis, also known as the polypody fern, is a hybrid species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are associated with several health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis × tricholepis include tricholomic acid, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pleopeltis × tricholepis is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Pleopeltis × tricholepis is a nothogenic fern species, which is the result of hybridization between two different species of the genus Pleopeltis. As it is a hybrid, its classification is complex and can be considered as a nothospecies. The classification of Pleopeltis × tricholepis is not universally agreed upon and may vary depending on the source. In some classifications, it is considered as a hybrid between Pleopeltis hirtella and Pleopeltis rufopilosa. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification of this fern species.
uses: Pleopeltis × tricholepis, a hybrid of the Polypodium tricholepis and Polypodium hesperium ferns, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Traditional uses of Pleopeltis × tricholepis include treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's extracts are thought to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
